sections.paragraph:
φρίκη, ης, ἡ (Soph., Hdt.+; SIG 1239, 19; Am 1:11) trembling caused by fear, a shudder (Eur. et al.; Plut., Arat. 32, 2; Job 4:14; TestSol 18:19 P; Jos., Bell. 5, 565; 6, 123) Hv 3, 1, 5 (w. τρόμος, as in the Job pass., also Philo, Leg. ad Gai. 267).—DELG s.v. φρίξ. Sv.

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Arat Arat of Soli in Cilicia, IV–III b.c.—List 5

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hv Hv = Visions

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1065
